intro: Callista bairdiana, also known as Wild Yam, is a perennial vine native to North America, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including relieving menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings, due to its estrogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Callista bairdiana include diosgenin and other steroidal saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
